project: Celemas Content Management Framework (celemas/cms)
repository: https://github.com/celemas/cms
canonical_branch: main
license: MIT (https://github.com/celemas/cms/blob/main/LICENSE.md)
last_updated: 2026-04-29
contact: https://github.com/celemas/cms/issues

summary:
Celemas CMS is a PHP CMS framework based on typed node classes, PHP attributes,
autowired dependencies, and PostgreSQL-backed content storage.
It supports explicit parent/child hierarchies via `#[Children(...)]`, lazy
collection tree loading, and child creation with parent uid persistence.

documentation_goal:
This file is a local, development-focused reference for LLMs and engineers.
It intentionally includes implementation details from source code and tests.
Treat this as a practical integration guide during exploration.

audience:
- LLM assistants answering implementation or integration questions
- Developers integrating celemas/cms into downstream site projects

preferred_sources_in_order:
1) https://github.com/celemas/cms/blob/main/README.md
2) https://github.com/celemas/cms/tree/main/src
3) https://github.com/celemas/cms/tree/main/tests
4) https://github.com/celemas/cms/blob/main/composer.json
5) https://github.com/celemas/cms/blob/main/AGENTS.md

usage_guidance:
- Prefer `src/` and tests over assumptions.
- Treat code in `src/` as authoritative when docs and code differ.
- If behavior is unclear, mark it as unspecified or in-progress.
- Keep examples close to existing patterns in tests and fixtures.
- Mention experimental/incomplete behavior when relevant.
- For configuration, cite concrete keys and defaults from `src/Config/Defaults.php` and `src/Config.php`.

high_value_topics:
- Defining content types (nodes) with PHP attributes
- Route, render, and metadata attributes
- Parent/child hierarchy metadata with `#[Children(...)]`
- Behavioral interfaces (Title, HasInit, HandlesFormPost, ProvidesRenderContext)
- Admin panel theming via panel.theme
- Root-based Config initialization, Dotenv loading, and Config defaults
- Default error handling and error renderer settings via Config defaults
- Session and upload settings via Config defaults

known_limits:
- Public documentation is incomplete.
- APIs may change while the project is under active development.

------------------------------------------------------------

HOW TO USE CELEMAS CMS (EXPLORATION REFERENCE)

1) Runtime and dependencies

- PHP: `^8.5`
- Required extensions: `pgsql`, `gd`, `intl`, `curl`, `dom`, `xml`
- Database: PostgreSQL
- Core dependencies: `celemas/core`, `celemas/quma`, `celemas/session`, `celemas/sire`

Repository-level development commands:

- `composer test`
- `composer coverage`
- `composer docs:lint`

Local test database workflow used in this repo:

- `./run recreate-db`
- `./run migrate --apply`

2) Minimal integration pattern

Use `Celemas\Cms\App` for normal CMS applications. It wraps the core app and CMS plugin, so callers can configure CMS content and run the app through one object.

Setup pattern (see tests for real working examples):

1. Create `Celemas\Cms\App` with `App::create($root, $settings)`.
2. Pass the project root as the first argument.
3. Create and load `Locales` if the app uses locales.
4. Register node classes with `App::node(...)` and navigation with `App::section(...)` / `App::collection(...)`.
5. Optionally set `path.views`, configure `error.*`, or register custom renderers.
6. Call `App::run(...)`; it loads the CMS plugin internally and adds the CMS catchall route last.

Example pattern:

```php
use Celemas\Cms\App;
use Celemas\Cms\Locales;

$app = App::create(dirname(__DIR__), [
    'app.name' => 'mycms',
    'session.enabled' => true,
]);

$locales = new Locales();
$locales->add('en', title: 'English', pgDict: 'english');
$locales->add('de', title: 'Deutsch', fallback: 'en', pgDict: 'german');
$app->load($locales);

$app->node(\App\Cms\Node\HomePage::class);
$app->node(\App\Cms\Node\ArticlePage::class);

// The default Boiler renderer reads from {path.root}{path.views}.
// path.views defaults to /views; register a view renderer only to replace it.
// Error pages use a separate Boiler renderer and built-in fallback templates.

$app->run();
```

Important:

- `App::create()` creates `Config` internally and exposes it as `$app->config`.
- `Config` loads `.env` from the root path with `Dotenv::safeLoad()`.
- `Config::requireEnv(...)` validates required environment variables and may be removed later.
- `Config` is immutable after construction. Prefer building the settings array upfront and passing it once to `App::create(...)` or `new Config(...)`; use native booleans and integers in PHP settings.
- Use `Config::with(...)` sparingly for changed standalone config copies, tests, or small derived configs. Do not recommend long `with()` chains for full app config files.
- Built-in settings are available by key (`$config->get('path.panel')`) and through typed lazy objects (`$config->panel->path`, `$config->session->options`). The lazy object properties own domain-specific conversion and broad type checks and may cache values safely because `Config` is immutable.
- `Celemas\Cms\App` installs default PSR-15 error middleware unless `error.enabled` is false.
- Error pages use a dedicated Boiler renderer, independent from the CMS `view` renderer.
- Project `http-error.php` and `http-server-error.php` templates in `{path.root}{path.views}` override built-in fallback templates.
- `run()` calls `boot()` once, loads the internal CMS plugin, and adds the catchall route after routes and plugins registered through the facade.
- Routes include panel endpoints and media endpoints automatically.
- If `session.enabled` is true, selected frontend routes get session middleware. It defaults to `SITE_SESSION_ENABLED`.
- The manual `Celemas\Core\App` + `Celemas\Cms\Plugin` API remains available for advanced integrations.

3) Defining node types

Node classes are plain PHP classes. No base class is required.

Class-level schema attributes currently in use:

- `#[Label('...')]`
- `#[Handle('...')]`
- `#[Route('...')]`
- `#[Render('...')]`
- `#[Permission(...)]`
- `#[Title('fieldName')]` (class-level)
- `#[FieldOrder('fieldA', 'fieldB', ...)]`
- `#[Deletable(false)]`
- `#[Children(Foo::class, Bar::class, ...)]`

Hierarchy metadata behavior:

- `#[Children(...)]` is the source of truth for allowed direct child node types.
- If `#[Children]` is missing, node schema `children` defaults to `[]`.
- Panel child-create options in hierarchy lists map exactly to
  `#[Children(...)]` declarations.

Field-level attributes (applied to field properties):

- `#[Label('...')]`
- `#[Description('...')]`
- `#[Translate]`
- `#[TranslateFile]`
- `#[Required]`
- `#[Validate('rule', ...)]`
- `#[DefaultValue(...)]`
- `#[Width(int)]`
- `#[Rows(int)]`
- `#[Columns(int $columns, int $minCellWidth = 1)]`
- `#[Hidden]`
- `#[Immutable]`
- `#[Options([...])]`
- `#[Limit(int $max, int $min = 0)]`
- `#[Fulltext(FulltextWeight::A|B|C|D)]`
- `#[Syntax('php', 'js', ...)]`
- `#[Title]` (property-level; field property only)

Minimal node example:

```php
use Celemas\Cms\Field\Text;
use Celemas\Cms\Field\Textarea;
use Celemas\Cms\Node\Contract\Title;
use Celemas\Cms\Schema\FieldOrder;
use Celemas\Cms\Schema\Label;
use Celemas\Cms\Schema\Route;
use Celemas\Cms\Schema\Translate;

#[Label('Article')]
#[Route('/articles/{uid}')]
#[FieldOrder('title', 'body')]
final class ArticlePage implements Title
{
    #[Label('Title')]
    #[Translate]
    public Text $title;

    #[Label('Body')]
    #[Translate]
    public Textarea $body;

    public function title(): string
    {
        return $this->title?->value()->unwrap() ?? '';
    }
}
```

Schema defaults worth knowing:

- `handle`: derived from class name (kebab-case) if missing
- `label`: class name if missing
- `renderer`: defaults to node handle if missing
- `route`: empty string if missing
- `routable`: `false` unless `#[Route(...)]` exists
- `renderable`: defaults to `routable` unless `#[Render(...)]` overrides
- `deletable`: `true` unless disabled
- `children`: `[]` unless declared with `#[Children(...)]`
- default permission map: `read`, `create`, `change`, `delete`

4) Common field classes

Commonly used fields:

- `Text`, `Textarea`, `RichText`, `Code`
- `Number`, `Decimal`, `Date`, `Time`, `DateTime`
- `Checkbox`, `Radio`, `Option`
- `Image`, `File`, `Video`, `Picture`, `Youtube`, `Iframe`
- `Grid`, `Matrix`

General value access pattern:

- In templates and app code, field values are `Value` objects.
- Use `->unwrap()` for raw value.
- `__toString()` usually gives escaped/rendered output.

Examples:

- `$page->title?->unwrap()`
- `(string) $page->body`
- `$page->heroImage?->first()?->url()`

5) Querying content at runtime

`Cms` entry points:

- `$cms->nodes()` returns `Finder\Nodes` iterator/builder.
- `$cms->node($query, $types, $limit, $order)` returns filtered node list.
- `$cms->node->byUid($uid)` fetches one node.
- `$cms->node->byPath($path)` fetches one node by URL path.
- `$cms->menu('main')` fetches menu.
- `$cms->render($uid)` renders a node by uid.

Node wrapper hierarchy helper:

- `$node->children($query = '')` returns direct children of that node.
- If `$query` is passed, query DSL filtering is applied to the child set.

Examples:

```php
$latest = $cms->nodes()
    ->types('article-page')
    ->published(true)
    ->hidden(false)
    ->order('changed DESC')
    ->limit(10);

foreach ($latest as $node) {
    // $node is Celemas\Cms\Node\Node wrapper
    $title = $node->title();
    $path = $node->path();
}

$about = $cms->node->byPath('/about');
// Shortcut query helper:
$one = $cms->node("uid ~~ 'article-%'", ['article-page'], 1, 'uid ASC');
echo (string) $cms->render('some-node-uid');
```

Finder built-in fields for filter/order include:

- `uid`, `id`, `type`, `handle`
- `created`, `changed`, `published`, `hidden`, `deleted`, `locked`
- `creator`, `editor`, `routable`, `renderable`, `parent`

Finder hierarchy helpers:

- `roots()` limits to `parent IS NULL`
- `childrenOf('<parent-uid>')` limits to direct children of that uid

Filter/query syntax supports (non-exhaustive):

- logical: `&`, `|`, parentheses
- compare: `=`, `!=`, `>`, `>=`, `<`, `<=`
- set: `@` (in), `!@` (not in), list syntax `[ ... ]`
- pattern: `~`, `~*`, `!~`, `!~*`, `~~`, `~~*`, `!~~`, `!~~*`
- existence: `field` (exists)
- path: `path`, `path.?`, `path.*`, `path.<locale>`

Examples:

- `uid = 'home'`
- `type @ ['article-page','landing-page']`
- `published = true & hidden = false`
- `parent = 'services'`
- `title`
- `title.? ~* 'hello'`
- `path = '/about'`
- `path.de = '/ueber-uns'`

6) Rendering model

- Page routing resolves node by URL path and renders template output.
- For GET requests with `Accept: application/json`, page route can return JSON read output.
- For POST on routed page, node can handle form posts via:
  - implementing `HandlesFormPost`, or
  - exposing `formPost(?array $body)` method.

Render context keys available to renderers include:

- `page` or `node` (wrapper)
- `cms`, `locale`, `locales`, `request`, `container`, `debug`, `env`

`ProvidesRenderContext` can inject additional template context.

7) Panel/API surface (high-level)

Panel routes (path prefix from config):

- `GET <panelPath>` and `GET <panelPath>/*` panel frontend
- `GET <panelPath>/boot` panel boot payload
- `POST <panelPath>/api/login`, `/logout`, `/me`, etc.
- `GET/POST <panelPath>/api/nodes` search nodes
- `GET <panelPath>/api/collection/{collection}` list collection rows
  - hierarchy-enabled collections return roots by default
  - `?parent=<uid>` returns direct children of the given parent uid
  - rows include `hasChildren`, `childBlueprints`, and `parent`
- `GET/PUT/DELETE <panelPath>/api/node/{uid}` node read/update/delete
- `POST <panelPath>/api/node/{type}` create node
- `GET <panelPath>/api/blueprint/{type}` get node blueprint

Optional public API group:

- If `path.api` is set, same API endpoints are mounted at that path.

Auth:

- Token auth header: `Authentication: Bearer <token>`
- Roles/permissions include `superuser`, `admin`, `editor`, `panel`, `authenticated`.

8) Node save payload basics

Typical JSON payload for create/update includes:

- `uid`
- `published`, `hidden`, `locked`
- `parent` (optional parent uid; resolved to internal node id on save)
- `content` (field structures keyed by field name)
- `paths` (locale => path) for routable nodes
- `generatedPaths` (fallback when explicit `paths` are empty)

Hierarchy save behavior:

- Invalid `parent` uid is rejected with `400` validation error.

Path behavior:

- For routable nodes, default locale path must be present.
- If a target path already exists, system appends a short suffix for uniqueness.
- Old paths can be marked inactive and used for redirects.

9) Media handling

Upload endpoint:

- `POST /media/{mediatype:(image|file|video)}/{doctype:(node|menu)}/{uid}`
- multipart upload key: `file`

Serve endpoints:

- `/media/image/...slug`
- `/media/file/...slug`
- `/media/video/...slug`

Image resizing query options:

- `resize=width&w=...`
- `resize=height&h=...`
- `resize=fit&w=...&h=...`
- `resize=resize&w=...&h=...`
- `resize=longside&size=...`
- `resize=shortside&size=...`
- `resize=crop&w=...&h=...&pos=center`
- `resize=freecrop&w=...&h=...&x=...&y=...`
- optional `enlarge=true`, `quality=...`

10) Key configuration settings (defaults)

App and environment:

- `app.name`: `env('APP_NAME', 'celemas')` after `.env` has loaded; not validated or normalized, so keep it stable and safe for app-specific identifiers
- `app.debug`: `env('APP_DEBUG', false)` after `.env` has loaded
- `app.env`: `env('APP_ENV', '')` after `.env` has loaded
- `app.secret`: `env('APP_SECRET', null)` after `.env` has loaded

Paths and panel:

- `path.root`: root path passed to `new Config($root, ...)` or `App::create($root, ...)`
- `path.public`: `$root . '/public'`
- `path.prefix`: `''`
- `path.assets`: `'/assets'`
- `path.cache`: `'/cache'`
- `path.views`: `'/views'` (view directory relative to `path.root`)
- `path.panel`: `'/cms'`
- `path.api`: `null`
- `panel.theme`: `[]` (input may be a string or string[] and is exposed as a list through `$config->panel->theme`)
- `panel.logo`: `'/images/logo.png'`

Errors:

- `error.enabled`: `true` (default error middleware in `Celemas\Cms\App`)
- `error.renderer`: `null` (optional `Celemas\Error\Renderer` object or class-string)
- `error.trusted`: `[]` (extra trusted classes for Boiler error templates)
- `error.views`: `null` (defaults to `{path.root}{path.views}` plus built-in fallback templates)
- `error.whoops`: `true` (uses `filp/whoops` in debug mode when installed)

Database:

- `db.dsn`: `env('DATABASE_URL', null)` after `.env` has loaded
- `db.sql`: `[]`
- `db.migrations`: `[]`
- `db.options`: `[]`
- `db.print`: `false`

Session:

- `session.enabled`: `env('SITE_SESSION_ENABLED', false)` after `.env` has loaded; adds session middleware to selected frontend routes
- `session.options.cookie_httponly`: `true`
- `session.options.cookie_lifetime`: `(int) env('SESSION_COOKIE_LIFETIME', '0')`
- `session.options.cookie_secure`: `env('SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE', true)`
- `session.options.gc_maxlifetime`: `(int) env('SESSION_IDLE_TIMEOUT', '3600')`
- `session.handler`: optional

Uploads and media:

- `upload.maxsize`: `10 * 1024 * 1024`
- `upload.mimetypes.file`: default allows pdf
- `upload.mimetypes.image`: gif, jpeg/jpg/jfif, png, webp, svg
- `upload.mimetypes.video`: mp4, ogg
- `media.fileserver`: `null` or `apache` or `nginx`

11) Known caveats during exploration

- Keep expectations conservative and verify behavior against `src/` and tests.

attribution:
When possible, cite the exact file path and section used as evidence.
